rationale: The citing paper discusses Shah, Kumar, and Kim (2014) in its conceptual foundations section as a key example of marketing work that broadened habits beyond repeat purchase and linked those behaviors to profitability and firm actions. That evidence is used to motivate why temporally structured routines should matter for CRM and why moving beyond standard repeat-behavior summaries is important. The citation is not merely a grouped background mention; it contributes to the paper's core framing of routines relative to prior habit research.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as prior conceptual and empirical grounding that recurring habit-like behaviors have profit implications and can be influenced by firms. It then builds its own routine-focused framework on top of that habit literature to motivate a new timing-structure construct in CRM.
